channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Duck Creek Launches Agentic AI Platform for Carriers
item.description: Duck Creek Technologies (Boston) has launched an insurance-native agentic AI platform and introduced new applications aimed at improving underwriting and claims operations for property/casualty carriers. “Our Agentic Platform combines our leadership in core systems, proprietary insurance domain ontology and expertise, and Agentic AI with neuro-symbolic reasoning to create agents that operate with full context, governance, traceability and human in the loop—so carriers can scale AI with confidence and trust.” Hardeep Gulati, CEO, Duck Creek Technologies. “Agentic AI will redefine how insurance operates—enabling carriers to move from manual, fragmented processes to orchestrated end-to-end decisioning and support for all personas to drive better outcomes and continuously improve,” comments Hardeep Gulati, Chief Executive Officer, Duck Creek.

item.title: Gramercy Risk Management Selects Origami Risk Core Platform
item.description: Gramercy Risk Management (Uniondale, NY.) has selected Origami Risk’s property/casualty platform from Origami Risk (Chicago) to support expansion and streamline core operations. “We’re honored to support Gramercy Risk Management as they take this important step in updating their core systems,” comments Mike Kaplan, Chief Client Officer, Origami Risk. “Origami Risk’s integrated, cloud-native platform gives us the flexibility to streamline operations across policy, billing, claims and risk management while supporting the launch of new programs and our evolving business needs.” Origami Risk describes its platform as providing a single environment for policy administration, rating, billing, claims, loss control, reporting, analytics, and digital engagement.

item.title: Purdue Pharma to Be Sentenced, Paving Way for Opioid Settlement
item.description: Related: Purdue Pharma Gets Court Nod for Bankruptcy Exit, Sackler Deal Purdue’s lawyer, Eli Vonnegut, said in court on Tuesday that the settlement fulfills the promises Purdue made in the bankruptcy litigation, even if victims of the opioid crisis are owed far more than the company can pay. Purdue Pharma appeared in New Jersey federal court on Tuesday for sentencing on charges of deceiving government regulators and paying kickbacks to doctors to boost opioid sales. Related: Purdue’s $5.5B Sentencing for Opioid Charges Delayed After Victims Show at Court Alexis Pleus, whose son Jeff died after he took opioids for a football injury and became addicted, told Arleo that justice would not be served by the settlement.

item.title: NYC Begins 10 Days of Flying Taxis With Manhattan-JFK Flights
item.description: The Santa Cruz, California-based company will be seeking to introduce New Yorkers to electric flights over 10 days of multiple trips. is planning to fly electric air taxis between John F. Kennedy International Airport and Manhattan this week to show off its quieter, zero-emission aircraft in a city weary of noise pollution.

item.title: Unexpected $115B+ Insured Loss Needed to Harden Property Market: Gallagher Re
item.description: “We now estimate that it would require a single event (or a series of large events) resulting in an insured loss of at least $115 billion to $125 billion – above the expected average annual catastrophe losses – to meaningfully impact the trajectory of pricing in the property sector of the industry,” Gallagher Re said. Q1 insured losses from natural disasters hit approximately $20 billion, agreed Aon and Gallagher Re in their Q1 nat cat market overviews. “The manageable start to 2026 continues a trend of lower loss quarters for insurers,” said Gallagher Re in its report titled “ Q1 2026 Gallagher Re Natural Catastrophe and Climate Report .” Gallagher Re said global insured losses of at least $20 billion were 26% below the decadal average of $26 billion.

item.title: ‘Catastrophic failure’ risk looms amid warning Shanghai floods could expand 80% by 2100
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Dallas Hail, Tornado Threats Are Grounding Hundreds of Flights
item.description: A powerful band of thunderstorms threatening softball-sized hail, tornadoes and dangerous winds are menacing Dallas and the US South, grounding hundreds of flights and knocking out power to thousands. local time, 278 flights were canceled at Dallas Fort Worth International Airport with dozens of others grounded in Chicago and Atlanta as the storms send ripples through US air traffic, according to FlightAware, an airline tracking service. Tornado watches stretch from northeast Texas to western Tennessee, and there are at least four reports of possible twisters moving across the region, according to the National Weather Service.

item.title: North Texas Tornadoes Leave 2 Dead and Destroy Multiple Homes
item.description: A tornado-producing thunderstorm left at least two people dead in northern Texas and displaced at least 20 families, with many homes sustaining major damage, authorities said Sunday. At least one person was killed and numerous homes were damaged Saturday night in the town of Runaway Bay, said Wise County Judge J.D. Emergency responders worked to clear debris to reach damaged homes and provide medical care where needed, Clark said.

item.title: Reassured hits 2M policy milestone
item.description: Reassured has reached two million life and critical illness policies sold since its 2009 launch, marking a milestone for one of the UK’s largest protection distributors. “Reassured is proud to reach this significant milestone of providing two million policies which give vital financial protection for families across the UK. Reassured attributes much of its growth to a digital strategy centered on AI and data.

item.title: Miller reports fifth year of growth
item.description: Miller reported another year of growth, with revenue rising 15% to £314 million for 2025, marking its fifth consecutive year of expansion. Growth was driven by a mix of acquisitions and geographic expansion. Miller acquired reinsurance broker AHJ, strengthening its treaty capabilities and adding exposure to Nordic, Caribbean, and North American markets.

item.title: Fintechs no vigiladas temen quedar fuera del open finance en Colombia
item.description: Las entidades no vigiladas por la Superintendencia Financiera de Colombia, entre ellas las fintechs de crédito digital, no estarán obligadas a implementar el open finance. Aunque buena parte de la industria es vigilada por la Superfinanciera, como por ejemplo los neobancos o las Sedpes (sociedades especializadas en depósitos y pagos electrónicos), Gabriel Santos, presidente ejecutivo de Colombia Fintech dijo a iupana que la participación de lo que la norma llama “terceros receptores de datos no vigilados” se realizará a través de un esquema voluntario. Así quedó definido en el Decreto 0368 que pone en marcha el sistema de finanzas abiertas en el país andino.

item.title: Neuro-Symbolic AI Addresses Insurance’s Explainability Challenge
item.description: Neuro-symbolic AI still has to solve the hardest challenge: building a structured knowledge layer from the unstructured documents real organizations actually have at enterprise scale. Gartner’s 2025 AI Hype Cycle names neuro-symbolic AI as the architecture designed for exactly this problem, automating decision-making with less risk of unintended consequences. Instead, neuro-symbolic architecture separates responsibilities.

item.title: Sony Defeats UK Suit Over Performers’ Rights for Classic Hendrix Albums
item.description: Sony Music Entertainment on Tuesday defeated a London lawsuit claiming performers’ property rights in relation to Jimi Hendrix’s classic 1960s albums, which the label had warned could have caused chaos for the music industry. London’s High Court ruled guitarist Noel Redding and drummer Mitch Mitchell effectively signed over their rights to The Jimi Hendrix Experience’s albums and rejected the claimants’ arguments that streaming was not covered by a 1966 deal. They sought a declaration that they owned a share of the sound recording copyrights of, and performers’ property rights in, the three Jimi Hendrix Experience albums.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Wants Hormuz Open in Tussle Over War’s End
item.description: Tehran wants the critical waterway for oil and gas shipments open “as soon as possible, as they try to figure out their leadership situation,” Trump said on Truth Social on Tuesday. President Donald Trump said Iran has asked the U.S. Iran has consistently said it will not open the strait as long as the US maintains its blockade.

item.title: Multiples Homes Destroyed, 2 Dead After Tornadoes Strike Northern Texas
item.description: A tornado-producing thunderstorm left at least two people dead in northern Texas and displaced at least 20 families, with many homes sustaining major damage, authorities said Sunday. At least one person was killed and numerous homes were damaged Saturday night in the town of Runaway Bay, said Wise County Judge J.D. Emergency responders worked to clear debris to reach damaged homes and provide medical care where needed, Clark said.

item.title: Thousands of Roundup Lawsuits Hinge on Bayer’s US Supreme Court Case
item.description: Supreme Court heard arguments on Monday in Bayer’s bid to limit thousands of lawsuits alleging that the German company’s Roundup weedkiller causes cancer in a case that is one part of sprawling yearslong litigation over the product. The justices are hearing Bayer’s appeal of a lower court’s ruling in a case brought by a man who said he was diagnosed with non-Hodgkin lymphoma, a type of blood cancer, after years of exposure to Roundup. The company is facing tens of thousands of similar lawsuits over Roundup in both federal and state courts across the country.

item.title: Ex-Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Extorting U.S. Victims, $10M in Assets Seized
item.description: A Florida man, formerly employed as a ransomware negotiator, pleaded guilty to conspiring to commit ransomware attacks against U.S. Working as a negotiator on behalf of five different ransomware victims, Martino provided BlackCat attackers with confidential information about the negotiating position and strategy of his company’s clients without the clients’ or his employer’s knowledge or permission, the agency said, adding that the confidential information assisted the ransomware actors and maximized the ransoms that the victims were required to pay. In addition, Martino admitted to conspiring with Ryan Goldberg of Georgia and Kevin Martin of Texas to successfully deploy BlackCat ransomware between April 2023 and November 2023 against multiple victims located throughout the United States.

item.title: Trust, tradition and the future of mutual growth
item.description: Working with others to solve difficult problems, protecting trust and good outcomes, and modernising where we can – so the framework supports growth and innovation. What defined the Enlightenment spirit was the refusal to settle, and a determination to make things better for the future. Speech by Sarah Pritchard, FCA deputy chief executive, at the BSA Annual Conference, Edinburgh.

item.title: Notable rise in ILS investor appetite and sophistication evident in 2026: Gallagher Re
item.description: A recent survey of insurance-linked securities (ILS) investors conducted by reinsurance broker Gallagher Re and Gallagher Securities reveals a notable rise in market appetite for 2026. Jason Bolding, global head of ILS at Gallagher Re, commented: “This is a unique moment: Capital conditions are favorable, investor appetite is strong and structures are evolving. The opportunity is to build relationships now that will matter when conditions tighten.” To gauge the mood among ILS investors in early 2026, both Gallagher Re and Gallagher Securities, the firm’s ILS and capital markets broker-dealer division, carried out a survey to gather investor perspectives, supplemented by insights obtained from a series of discussions with expert allocators.

item.title: VIG’s 2025 result rises by 32% to €1.2bn
item.description: Vienna Insurance Group (VIG) has reported solid financials for the full year of 2025, as result before taxes grew by 31.7% to €1.16 billion, compared to €881.8 million for the full year of 2024. The insurer’s “strong” growth for FY’25 was driven by an expansion in gross written premiums (GWP) of 7.1% to €16.3 billion, and the aforementioned positive result before taxes. Additionally, the Group’s solvency ratio for 2025 was 296%, due to very strong capitalisation.

item.title: Re/insurance industry veteran Dan Malloy joins Banyan Risk as Executive Managing Director
item.description: Veteran insurance and reinsurance executive and former CEO of United Risk, SiriusPoint, and Third Point Re, Dan Malloy, joins specialty MGA Banyan Risk on May 1st, 2026, as Executive Managing Director. With four decades of industry experience, including senior executive and leadership positions across a range of brokers, insurers, and reinsurers, Malloy joins Banyan Risk as the firm embarks on its next phase of growth and further scales its underwriting platform. In his new role, Malloy will work closely with Banyan Risk’s co‑founders and CEOs, Tim Usher-Jones and Peter Horrobin, and the MGA’s leadership team.

item.title: Cincinnati generates $274m Q1’26 net income
item.description: US primary insurer Cincinnati Financial Corporation has announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2026, reporting a net income of $274 million, compared to a net loss of $90 million in Q1 2025. The primary driver for the net income increase was a $326 million boost from property casualty underwriting profit, and $31 million from investment income. Non-GAAP operating income stood at $330 million, compared with an operating loss of $37 million in Q1 last year.

item.title: Lockton Unveils Plans for Future Global Headquarters in Leawood, Kansas
item.description: Lockton revealed new details this week of its future global headquarters located in Leawood, Kansas. Lockton first announced the headquarters project in December 2025. It’s a future-focused destination designed to elevate how we serve clients and how we support our people,” said Ron Lockton, Chairman and CEO of Lockton.

item.title: Register: Risky Future AI Tools for Commercial Auto, Telematics & Fleet Risks on April 29
item.description: In partnership with Insurance Journal, Risky Future’s AI Tools for Commercial Auto, Telematics & Fleet Risks Demo Day will be held on Wednesday, April 29, beginning at 1pm ET. Insurance Journal’s Risky Future series is hosting the “ Commercial Auto, Telematics & Fleet Risks ” Demo Day, a series of free AI tool demonstrations designed exclusively for commercial auto carriers, fleet underwriters, risk managers, brokers and more. TruckerCloud: TruckerCloud is the leading telematics platform for commercial auto insurance, trusted by the majority of the industry’s top carriers.

item.title: Georgia Blaze Shows How Climate Change Has Led to More Wildfires in the East
item.description: Often considered more a problem for Western North America, wildfires are becoming more intense, frequent and damaging in the East, such as this week’s blaze that destroyed dozens of homes in Georgia, fire scientists said. Researchers blame a number of factors including climate change causing fuel to dry out and be more flammable, a record drought, tens of millions of tons of dead trees from Hurricane Helene and just the large area where dense forests and high numbers of people try to coexist. So far this year, 2,802 square miles (7,258 square kilometers) of the United States has burned in wildfires — much of it in Nebraska, an unusual area for massive wildfires — that’s 88% more than the 10-year average for this time of year, according to the National Interagency Fire Center.

item.title: Duck Creek launches agentic AI platform to power next-generation insurance operations
item.description: The post Duck Creek launches agentic AI platform to power next-generation insurance operations appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Duck Creek Technologies has introduced a new insurance-native Agentic AI Platform, designed to bring advanced underwriting and claims capabilities into a unified, intelligent framework across the insurance lifecycle. “Agentic AI will redefine how insurance operates, enabling carriers to move from manual, fragmented processes to orchestrated end-to-end decisioning and support for all personas to drive better outcomes and continuously improve,” said Hardeep Gulati, Chief Executive Officer at Duck Creek.

item.title: Jump partners with Markel to expand AI tools and insurance support for financial advisors
item.description: The post Jump partners with Markel to expand AI tools and insurance support for financial advisors appeared first on Insurtech Insights . “By bringing together Jump’s technology and Markel’s insurance capabilities, we’re able to offer advisors more tailored, forward-looking solutions that support both their growth and their risk management needs.” – Joseph Caruso, managing director of Joseph Caruso & Associates. Jump, an AI productivity platform for financial advisors, has entered into a partnership with Markel to broaden access to its technology across Markel’s network of financial professionals while supporting advisory firms as they adopt AI-driven workflows.

item.title: Where AI is already speeding up agency workflows
item.description: Where AI can make an impact now AI will make the biggest difference for large agencies in workflows that are repetitive, manual, and difficult to scale. But the tools to change your agency are here now. Inefficient processes, disconnected data, or siloed workflows scale, as well, and can lead to logjams in everyday work.

item.title: City of San Diego selects Klear.ai
item.description: Klear.ai has partnered with the City of San Diego to modernize the city’s claims administration, risk management, and safety operations. The City of San Diego, the eighth largest city in the U.S., selected Klear.ai following a competitive evaluation process. “This partnership is built on shared ambition — combining cycle-wide automation and advanced business intelligence to deliver real efficiency gains and give leadership the visibility they need to protect San Diego’s workforce and community.” — Pete Govek, Chief Revenue Officer, Klear.ai.

item.title: Ki adds Tokio Marine Kiln to expand digital follow capacity
item.description: Tokio Marine Kiln is joining as Ki’s fifth capacity partner, using the platform to deploy targeted follow capacity across select classes. Ki Insurance added new capacity to its digital follow platform, expanding the size of lines brokers can access across the Lloyd’s market. Ki’s model allows carriers to allocate capacity in a controlled way using algorithmic underwriting, with a focus on follow business.

item.title: Seguro de salud para autónomos: ventajas fiscales y coberturas clave
item.description: Desde la perspectiva del trabajador por cuenta propia, el seguro médico para autónomos ofrece ventajas adicionales ligadas a desgravaciones fiscales que permiten aligerar el gasto futuro en impuestos, a lo que se suma la comodidad de la sanidad privada. El ahorro en impuestos es, para muchos autónomos, el empujón definitivo a la hora de decantarse por contratar una póliza de salud. Más allá del ahorro fiscal, hay otros motivos de peso para contratar un seguro de salud siendo autónomo.

item.title: Executive View: AI Strategy in Insurance Requires Plug-and-Play Operating Model
item.description: The insurance industry is entering a phase of artificial intelligence (AI) adoption where the primary constraint is no longer capability. Most core use cases, including submission intake, underwriting support, claims triage, document processing, and customer service augmentation, have already been developed. Many carriers are not deciding whether AI can be applied but determining which solutions merit implementation and how those decisions should be managed over time.

item.title: From promise to practice: shaping open finance policy with our Smart Data Accelerator
item.description: That’s the question we want to answer with our Smart Data Accelerator , which enables firms to showcase open finance solutions in a digital testing environment to help shape policy making. Because of this interdependency, developing open finance won’t come down to a single policy intervention or technical change. But to make that promise a reality, we need to look at how it works in practice.

item.title: FCA invites ESG rating providers to join a voluntary reporting pilot
item.description: We're inviting ESG rating providers to join a pilot to inform future regulatory reporting once the regime is live. The pilot is open to all ESG rating providers who expect to be in scope of UK regulation. The pilot aims to help us assess whether the proposed metrics for ESG ratings reporting are: Participants will have a direct opportunity to inform: Based on your feedback, we may revise the metrics for the eventual reporting regime.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck On How Startup Founders Can Build Moats In Vertical AI
item.description: As a partner at New Enterprise Associates , Tiffany Luck invests in the AI application layer and B2B SaaS. Today, Luck’s thesis centers on the AI application layer, betting on vertical AI and the “last mile” of automation to bridge the gap between horizontal model potential and tangible enterprise ROI. We recently spoke with Luck about the increasing relevance of vertical AI, how startups can carve out durable advantages in a world dominated by platform giants and more.

item.title: Expanding the ILS perimeter – New risks, markets, capital: ILS NYC 2026 video
item.description: The next video from the event that we are publishing is from a panel session focused on the broadening of the asset class, titled “Expanding the ILS perimeter: New risks, new markets, new capital.” This ILS market expansion focused panel discussion was kindly moderated by Kathleen Faries , CEO, Artex Capital Solutions. Our latest video features another panel discussion from Artemis ILS NYC 2026, a session focused on the expanding range of investment opportunities in the ILS asset class , from our tenth catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) conference in the ILS NYC series, held on February 6th 2026 in New York. She was joined on stage by: Joanna Syroka , Director of New Markets, Fermat Capital Management, LLC; Chris McKeown , Chief Executive, Reinsurance, ILS & Innovation, Vantage Risk; Danny Arnett , Managing Director, Insurance Linked Securities, Ryan Specialty Underwriting Managers; and Bob Forness , CEO, MultiStrat Group.

item.title: Casualty ILS transparency must match nat-cat standards: Allphins
item.description: Whilst the casualty insurance-linked securities (ILS) space continues to emerge as a transformative capital source for long-tail risk, data-and-analytics specialist Allphins argues that ILS investors in casualty risk deserve the same level of transparency in exposure and systemic risk understanding that natural catastrophe investors have long been receiving. But ILS investors in casualty risk deserve a level of transparency in exposure and systemic risk understanding that nat‑cat investors have long received,” Allphins said in a recent article based on its interview with Yulia Bruskova, CEO and Founder of Cadence Partners. Allphins additionally observes that casualty ILS investors seek to understand measures of portfolio diversification and volatility influenced by correlation clusters and behavioral dynamics, along with forward-looking indicators instead of historical averages, and a clear representation of exposure characteristics in relation to frequency and severity outcomes.

item.title: Diot-Siaci Group revenue increases 28% in 2025
item.description: The Diot-Siaci Group, an independent insurance and reinsurance brokerage, has announced gross revenue of €1.31 billion for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. In November 2025, The Diot-Siaci Group finalised a new ownership structure, with long-standing shareholder, the Burrus Group, increasing its stake from 42% to 47%. Diot-Siaci Corporate Solutions, one of the Group’s business units and the entity primarily dedicated to Property & Casualty (P&C) activities for Key Accounts and the Middle market in France, recorded gross revenue of €287 million, up by more than 5% compared with €274 million in 2024, supported by strong commercial momentum across most business lines, particularly in the Middle market segment.

item.title: CFC names Isabel Finn as Head of Cyber Exposure and Proactive Risk
item.description: CFC, a specialist insurance provider centred on emerging risks and recognised as a market leader in cyber insurance, has appointed Isabel Finn as Head of Cyber Exposure and Proactive Risk. That’s increasingly what the market is asking for, and this role is a key step in delivering it.” Finn brings experience in cyber security, exposure analysis and risk translation, with a track record of helping insurers, underwriters and security leaders understand how technical cyber risks translate into real-world losses and how they can be reduced through better insight and prioritisation. Before joining CFC, Finn was Cyber Services Manager at Canopius, where she oversaw cyber exposure and service initiatives designed to support underwriting and risk reduction.

item.title: PPL introduces updated trading console to advance digital placement capabilities
item.description: Placing Platform Limited (PPL), a London-based company providing digital trading solutions for the insurance sector, has released an updated version of its trading console, developed in partnership with Microsoft. The company positions it as a primary channel for delivering future enhancements, including expanded functionality, improved connectivity and additional services, as digital trading adoption increases across the market. PPL states that the console introduces tools designed to support more effective planning and quicker responses.

item.title: Viewpoint: Insurance’s Data Problem Comes Into Focus at Hormuz
item.description: In recent weeks, the Strait of Hormuz has forced the marine insurance market into real-time decision making. Over the past several years, the insurance industry has invested heavily in artificial intelligence, particularly in tools that extract structured data from unstructured documents. Executive Summary: Rapidly shifting war-risk conditions are exposing structural weaknesses in delegated authority underwriting, according to Alex Babin, CEO of Hercules Systems, who notes that many underwriters still rely on delayed, inconsistent bordereaux data.

item.title: Renewables in Vogue as Iran War Drives Up Europe Power Prices
item.description: Albania is an example of how countries with a higher renewables output have been protected from steep rises in electricity prices since the United States and Israel attacked Iran on February 28, price comparisons from across Europe show. As the Iran war disrupts global flows of oil and gas and energy prices skyrocket, the Drin River, which descends through the mountains of northern Albania, is acting as a kind of shield. Swelled by winter rains and snowmelt, and dotted with hydroelectric dams built during communist times, the river’s power provides more than 90% of the Balkan country’s electricity output, helping to keep wholesale prices in check.

item.title: WTW strengthens AI strategy with senior leadership appointments
item.description: Willis Towers Watson (WTW) has appointed two senior leaders to accelerate its enterprise-wide artificial intelligence strategy, naming Spike Lipkin as chief AI officer and Gordon Wintrob as head of AI acceleration, effective immediately. The appointments build on WTW’s acquisition of Newfront in January 2026 and are designed to scale the insurtech’s AI-native capabilities across the global organisation. His background in building one of the industry’s largest AI-powered broking platforms, alongside his involvement in integrating Newfront into WTW, positions him to guide how AI is embedded across the company’s strategy, operating model, and culture.

item.title: Marsh and Formula 1 announce multi-year partnership
item.description: Marsh announced a multi-year partnership with Formula 1 as its first official risk and insurance brokering partner. The deal gives Marsh trackside branding and access to events across Formula 1’s global calendar. “Formula 1 embodies the pinnacle of performance, precision and resilience—values that are deeply aligned with Marsh’s commitment to helping clients navigate complexity and unlock opportunity.

item.title: Northwestern Mutual announces new $150 million fund to invest in startups
item.description: Northwestern Mutual announced a new $150 million investment to support startups via Northwestern Mutual Future Ventures, which will expand the company’s total venture capital allocation to $350 million. The funds expand our ability to partner with high-growth companies, delivering technology, collaboration, and value to our more than five million clients and our nationwide network of trusted financial professionals.” – Michael Sias, vice president – corporate development and venture, Northwestern Mutual. The $150 million infusion to NMFV – known as Fund III – will target emerging and growth-stage firms in the fintech and insurtech space.

item.title: Protective to acquire Obsidian
item.description: Protective Life has entered into an agreement with private equity firm Genstar Capital to acquire property, casualty and specialty insurance platform Obsidian Insurance Holdings and its affiliates. By bringing Obsidian into Protective, we are adding a high-quality specialty insurance platform that expands where and how we grow while staying true to our vision to protect more people through life’s many moments. We look forward to welcoming Obsidian’s team upon closing.” – Rich Bielen, Chief Executive Officer of Protective.

item.title: La rentabilidad en el mercado medio se estrecha
item.description: El informe señala que la rentabilidad de estas carteras se está viendo presionada por años de infratarificación , un uso limitado de herramientas de tarificación basada en datos y el deterioro de los ratios de siniestralidad, en un contexto donde la competencia y las expectativas de los corredores dificultan subir tarifas o endurecer condiciones. El estudio estima que una ejecución coordinada podría elevar los resultados técnicos brutos un 30%-50% , principalmente mediante reducción, transferencia de exposiciones elevadas y recuperación de rentabilidad en pólizas “intermedias”. Las aseguradoras que operan en el segmento midmarket -líneas comerciales como Daños y Responsabilidad Civil para empresas con ingresos entre 20 y 200 millones de euros- afrontan un entorno más complejo en el que el crecimiento exige mayor control técnico, según un análisis de McKinsey .

item.title: Transparencia Retributiva: Cómo la Directiva (UE) 2023/970 fomenta el liderazgo femenino y la equidad corporativa
item.description: Por ello, se ha desarrollado la Directiva (UE) 2023/970, aprobada en mayo de 2023, que busca pasar de la opacidad salarial a la transparencia obligatoria como palanca para el cambio. La conexión entre esta Directiva y el liderazgo femenino no es cosmética. «La conexión entre esta Directiva y el liderazgo femenino no es cosmética.

item.title: Solo el 18% de las empresas logra capturar el valor real de la IA: la clave está en el talento
item.description: Para Sandra Moreno, managing director de Talento en Accenture España y Portugal, el reto no es únicamente tecnológico: “solo el 18% de las empresas está materializando el valor de la IA al rediseñar cómo se realiza el trabajo y capacitar a las personas. Así lo refleja el informe “Talent Reinventors: Creando valor con y para las personas en la era de la IA” de Accenture , que señala que solo el 18% de las empresas integra de forma efectiva su estrategia empresarial con el talento y la tecnología para desbloquear el potencial de la IA. Esta falta de conexión entre tecnología y personas está generando una brecha creciente entre la adopción de la IA y la creación de valor real para el negocio.

item.title: Medtronic Says Cyberattack on IT Network Has Not Disrupted Operations
item.description: Medtronic said the attack that hit the network supporting its corporate IT systems did not impact its products, patient safety, manufacturing or distribution operations. The IT network remain separate from those that support its products, manufacturing and distribution operations, Medtronic said on Friday. Medical device maker Medtronic said on Monday a cyberattack on its computer systems last week did not affect its products or ability to meet patient needs, and is not expected to materially impact its business or financial results.
